headline: PE & Healthcare Deals Heating Up While Operating Partners Become the MVPs

stories.title: CPA Firms Are The PE Playground in 2026
stories.summary: Almost half of the top 30 US CPA firms now have PE backing, fr. Blackstone's $2B Citrin Cooperman deal just proved PE can actually exit these platforms—game changer for the whole sector.
stories.takeaway: PE is aggressively consolidating professional services; if you're covering this space, know the playbook around roll-ups and tech-enabled M&A.

stories.title: Healthcare M&A Bouncing Back Harder—Tech-Enabled Assets Are the Prize
stories.summary: Health services dealmaking is rebounding with mega-funds flexing and mid-market PE finally getting competitive again. AI-driven platforms, behavioral health, and carve-outs are lowkey where the best multiples are.
stories.takeaway: Buyers are paying premiums for assets with digital infrastructure & measurable clinical upside—diligence on tech capabilities is now table stakes.

stories.title: To Get Ahead in Consulting, Build Your Operating Partner Credentials Now
stories.summary: PE firms are hunting for operating partners with AI, digital transformation, and supply chain expertise—not just analysts doing models. The shift from analysis to execution is real and compensation is following.
stories.takeaway: If you want to move fast in PE consulting, start building deep operational expertise in one sector now. That's the differentiator in 2026.

watchNext.title: Carve-Out Strategy Evolution: From Price to Performance in 2026
watchNext.why: Carve-outs are becoming THE deal strategy for PE—understanding IT separation, tech stacks, and value creation beyond the purchase price is essential for any analyst.

watchNext.title: How AI Adoption is Actually Creating PE Premiums (Not Just Hype)
watchNext.why: AI went from differentiator to table stakes—see how leading PE firms are embedding it into diligence, portfolio operations, and exit planning.
